hero.embedded_thesis: At A$3.56, the current price embeds a scenario between the base case and the bear case – the market is pricing a 12-18 month delay to first commercial revenue, a non-trivial probability of a dilutive capital raise before profitability, and an ongoing short-seller overhang from Spruce Point's hinted second report. Today's +9.5% rebound from the March 19 close of approximately A$3.25 suggests some short covering and opportunistic buying, but the stock remains approximately 49% below its March 12 high of A$6.93, indicating the market has not restored confidence in the prior growth narrative. The US$65.85 million cash position and US$60 million+ in government funding are receiving limited credit at current levels, as investors focus on the widening H1 2026 net loss of US$34.77 million and the accounting correction that triggered the sell-off. Broad commodity weakness – copper down 4.8% and gold down 6.6% today – adds a sector-level headwind that further suppresses any near-term re-rating.
hero.skew_description: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with N2 (base case neutral grind, 37%) carrying the highest weight, appropriate for a development-stage company with adequate but depleting cash runway. N3 (bear case, 33%) carries unusually high weight, reflecting the cluster of negative signals: accounting correction, widening losses, short-seller activity, and heavy selling volume. The skew is modestly bearish relative to a standard initiation, with N1 and N4 combined at only 30%, reflecting the absence of near-term revenue catalysts and the unresolved Spruce Point threat.

next_decision_point.metric: Whether Spruce Point publishes a second report, the substance of any new allegations beyond the known accounting correction, and the quality and speed of IperionX's rebuttal
next_decision_point.thresholds: Positive shift: Spruce Point does not publish, or publishes and IperionX provides a credible, independently verified rebuttal – removes a major overhang and shifts weight toward N2/N1. Negative shift: Spruce Point publishes with substantive new allegations (undisclosed related-party transactions, technology claims, or grant eligibility questions) that the company cannot credibly rebut – accelerates de-rating toward N3 and A$1.80-A$2.50 range.

heroCompanyDescription: IperionX Limited (ASX: IPX) is an Australian-listed, US-focused critical minerals and advanced materials company developing a domestic titanium supply chain in the United States. Formerly known as Hyperion Metals Limited, the company uses patented metal technologies to produce performance titanium alloys from titanium minerals or scrap titanium at lower energy, cost, and carbon emissions than conventional processes.

skew.rationale: The probability distribution is skewed bearish at initiation, with N3 (downside) and N2 (neutral grind) together accounting for 70% of the probability mass. The accounting correction, accelerating cash burn, Spruce Point overhang, and heavy institutional selling volume in the week of March 16-19, 2026 represent a cluster of negative signals that outweigh the structural onshoring tailwind in the near term. The bull case (N1 at 20%) requires multiple simultaneous positive outcomes that are not yet in evidence.

verdict.text: At A$3.56, IperionX is priced for a prolonged pre-revenue grind with meaningful downside risk – sitting approximately 49% below its March 12 high of A$6.93 and only modestly above our bear case of A$1.80. Today's +9.5% rebound is a notable single-day move but does not resolve the fundamental concerns: the company reported a US$34.77 million net loss for H1 2026 (more than double the prior year), remains pre-revenue with zero commercial sales, and faces an active short-seller threat from Spruce Point. The US$65.85 million cash position and US$60 million+ in government funding provide runway but are receiving limited market credit given the burn rate and governance questions raised by the accounting correction. Our assessment is that the current price is approximately fair given the evidence, sitting between our base case of A$3.50 and our bear case of A$1.80, with the balance of risk skewed modestly to the downside until the Q2 2026 DFS provides a credible path to first commercial revenue.

featuredRationale: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with N2 (base case neutral grind, 38%) carrying the highest weight as is appropriate for initial coverage of a development-stage company. N3 (bear case, 32%) carries unusually high weight for a base-case initiation, reflecting the cluster of negative near-term signals. N1 (bull case, 20%) and N4 (tail catalyst, 10%) together represent 30% upside probability, anchored by the genuine strategic value of the onshoring thesis and government funding validation.

identity.overview: IperionX Limited (ASX: IPX) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: IperionX completes its Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS) in Q2 2026 with strong economics, secures additional US government offtake or grant commitments, and begins generating first commercial revenue from its Virginia facility within 12 months. Today's +9.5% rebound to A$3.56 proves to be the beginning of a sustained recovery as the market recognises the March sell-off as an overreaction to a corrected accounting error rather than a fundamental deterioration in the business.
supporting: US$60M+ in secured government funding provides credible institutional validation of the onshoring thesis
Q2 2026 DFS completion is a near-term hard catalyst that could reset the narrative
7x capacity expansion at Virginia facility signals management confidence in commercial scale-up
Structural tailwind from US push to onshore titanium manufacturing and reduce China supply chain dependency
Cash position of US$65.85M provides runway to reach first commercial sales without immediate dilution
contradicting: Company remains pre-revenue with zero commercial sales despite years of development, raising timeline credibility questions
Net loss widened to US$34.77M in H1 2026 from US$16.24M a year earlier, accelerating cash burn
Spruce Point hinting at a second short-seller attack could suppress re-rating even if fundamentals improve
Accounting correction to H1 results undermines confidence in financial governance at a critical juncture
Broader commodity price weakness and World Bank six-year-low forecast create a difficult macro backdrop

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: IperionX continues its pre-revenue development trajectory, with the DFS completing in Q2 2026 and the Virginia facility 7x capacity expansion progressing on schedule, but first commercial revenue remains 12-18 months away. The stock stabilises in the A$3.00-A$4.50 range as the market digests the H1 2026 net loss of US$34.77 million, the accounting correction, and the short-seller overhang, while the US$65.85 million cash position and US$60 million+ in government funding provide adequate runway.
supporting: US$65.85M cash and US$60M+ government funding provide a credible 12-18 month operational runway
DFS completion in Q2 2026 is a defined near-term milestone that could provide incremental positive newsflow
Dual listing on ASX and Nasdaq broadens the investor base and provides access to US capital markets
Patented metal technologies for lower-energy, lower-cost titanium production represent a genuine differentiated asset
contradicting: Accounting correction and widening losses have damaged near-term sentiment and invited short-seller scrutiny
Zero commercial sales after years of development raises questions about the pace of commercialisation
Heavy selling volume (144% above 90-day average) on March 19 suggests institutional distribution, not just retail panic
World Bank commodity price forecast at six-year lows creates a difficult environment for mining/materials re-ratings

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: The accounting correction, widening losses, and Spruce Point's hinted second short-seller attack combine to sustain a de-rating below A$3.00. The market concludes that IperionX's financial controls are inadequate, the commercialisation timeline is being repeatedly pushed out, and the government funding narrative overstates near-term revenue visibility. A dilutive capital raise before first commercial revenue, combined with a disappointing or delayed DFS, drives the stock toward the A$1.50-A$2.00 range.
supporting: Accounting correction issued just two days after initial H1 filing raises serious internal controls concerns
Spruce Point Capital hinting at a second short-seller report is a material overhang on the stock
Net loss nearly doubled year-on-year to US$34.77M with zero commercial revenue to offset burn
Heavy volume selling (144% above average) on March 19 suggests informed sellers, not just noise
Stock already down 42% from March high of A$6.93 to current A$3.60, with momentum firmly negative
Section 708A notice in February 2026 suggests recent capital raising, implying potential ongoing dilution risk
contradicting: US$65.85M cash position and US$60M+ government grants reduce near-term insolvency risk
Company denied undisclosed negative news in ASX price query response, suggesting no hidden catastrophic event
US government strategic interest in titanium onshoring provides a political backstop to funding continuity
DFS completion in Q2 2026 could provide a positive reset if economics are compelling

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A major US defence contractor, aerospace prime, or strategic acquirer recognises IperionX's patented low-cost titanium technology and Virginia facility as a critical national security asset and moves to acquire or enter a transformative offtake agreement. Alternatively, a significant expansion of US government funding or a new executive order mandating domestic titanium sourcing triggers a re-rating to A$10.00+, consistent with the stock's March 12 high of A$9.10 on the 52-week range.
supporting: US government has already committed US$60M+ in grants, demonstrating strategic interest at the highest level
Patented technology for lower-energy, lower-cost titanium from scrap or minerals is strategically valuable to defence supply chains
US-China trade tensions and critical mineral supply chain concerns are intensifying, raising the strategic premium on domestic titanium capacity
Dual Nasdaq/ASX listing increases visibility to US strategic and institutional investors
contradicting: Company is pre-revenue with no demonstrated commercial-scale production, making acquisition valuation highly uncertain
Accounting correction and short-seller scrutiny would complicate any due diligence process
No current public signals of M&A interest or strategic partnership discussions
Widening losses and governance questions reduce attractiveness as an acquisition target at current development stage

narrative.theNarrative: [Updated 20-Mar-26 15:20 AEST] IperionX entered March 2026 riding a powerful narrative: a US-government-backed, patented-technology titanium producer positioned at the intersection of the onshoring megatrend and critical minerals security, with over US$60 million in grant funding and a 7x Virginia capacity expansion underway. That narrative was severely stress-tested in the space of one week. The release of H1 2026 accounts on March 11, followed by a correction just two days later on March 13, triggered a 22% single-day collapse on March 16 and a further 9.14% decline on March 19, taking the stock from A$6.93 to approximately A$3.25 – a 53% peak-to-trough collapse in under two weeks. The corrected H1 2026 accounts revealed a net loss of US$34.77 million, more than double the prior year's US$16.24 million, with the company remaining pre-revenue with zero commercial sales. Short sellers, led by Spruce Point Capital hinting at a second attack, circled aggressively on volume running 144% above the 90-day average on March 19. Today's +9.5% rebound to A$3.56 provides some relief and may reflect short covering, but the stock remains deeply below its March highs and the fundamental picture – pre-revenue, widening losses, accounting governance questions – has not materially changed. The next critical test is the Q2 2026 DFS completion, which must deliver credible economics and a defined path to first commercial production to restore the growth narrative.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$3.56
priceImplication.content:
At A$3.56, the current price appears to embed:

- A commercialisation timeline of 18-24 months to first commercial revenue, materially longer than the market was pricing at the March 12 high of A$6.93
- A non-trivial probability (estimated 30-40%) of a dilutive capital raise before first revenue, given the H1 2026 burn rate of approximately US$34.77M over six months against a cash position of US$65.85M
- Ongoing short-seller overhang from Spruce Point's hinted second report, suppressing any near-term re-rating
- Reduced confidence in financial governance following the March 13 accounting correction, described by media as a 'typo blunder'
- Limited credit for the US$60M+ in government funding and the strategic value of the Virginia facility's 7x capacity expansion
- A sector-level headwind from broad commodity weakness (copper -4.8%, gold -6.6% today), which dampens risk appetite for pre-revenue critical minerals names
- Today's +9.5% bounce is consistent with short covering rather than a fundamental re-rating at this stage

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base is moderate in breadth but thin in depth. Corporate communications (ASX announcements) and regulatory filings provide a verified factual foundation, including the corrected H1 2026 net loss of US$34.77 million, cash position of US$65.85 million, and government funding quantum of US$60 million+. The accounting correction – filed just two days after the original H1 accounts – is a verified negative data point that the market has priced aggressively. Short-seller activity (Spruce Point hinting at a second report) is corroborated by multiple independent media sources including Capitalbrief.com and Kalkine, lending it credibility as an ongoing overhang. The company's ASX price query response on March 16 denied undisclosed news, which is a factual statement but provides limited reassurance given the market's continued selling. Counterbalancing evidence is thin: the government funding and Virginia expansion are well-documented but have not been updated with new milestones since the H1 release. Today's +9.5% rebound is observable market data but its cause (short covering vs. genuine buying) is unverified. The macro backdrop – broad commodity weakness and geopolitical disruption in aluminium markets – is directionally negative for sector sentiment but not specifically material to titanium pricing. On balance, the evidence modestly supports the bear-to-base skew in current hypothesis weights.
narrative.narrativeStability: The dominant narrative – development-stage onshoring bet under severe short-term pressure – is fragile and highly event-dependent. The March sell-off has already occurred and the accounting correction has been filed; those events are now in the past and the narrative has absorbed them. The stability of the current narrative now rests on two near-term tests: first, whether Spruce Point publishes a second short-seller report (expected April 2026), which could either be a decisive negative catalyst or, if rebutted credibly, remove a major overhang; and second, whether the Q2 2026 DFS delivers economics that restore credibility to the commercialisation timeline. Today's +9.5% rebound has not resolved either of these uncertainties. A second Spruce Point report with substantive new allegations – beyond the accounting correction already known – would likely break the narrative toward N3. Conversely, a DFS with NPV materially above the current market cap and a defined 12-month path to first revenue would shift weight decisively toward N1. The narrative is not self-sustaining at current levels; it requires a positive catalyst to prevent gradual drift toward the bear case as cash burn continues.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality – from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary – ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: IperionX Limited responded to an ASX price query on March 16, 2026, denying any undisclosed news behind a sharp share price fall from A$6.93 on March 12, 2026, to an intraday low of A$4.58 on March 16, 2026. The company highlighted its US grant funding and ongoing strategic developments, as confirmed by the ASX announcement on March 16, 2026.
cards.tension: Despite the company's denial, the share price continued its decline, plunging approximately 42% from its recent March high of $6.93 by March 19, 2026, suggesting the market was not fully reassured. However, the stock saw a +9.5% increase today, potentially indicating a short-term rebound or a pause in the negative sentiment.
cards.source: ASX: Response to ASX Price Query (2026-03-16); TipRanks (2026-03-16); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-19)

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: IperionX reported Half Year Accounts on March 11, 2026, which were subsequently corrected on March 13, 2026, as confirmed by ASX announcements. The corrected accounts for H1 2026 (to December 31, 2025) showed a net loss widening to US$34.77 million, up from US$16.24 million a year earlier, with basic and diluted loss per share at US$0.10. News headlines on simplywall.st and stocksdownunder.com reiterated these sharply wider losses.
cards.tension: The significant widening of losses is attributed to heavy reinvestment in infrastructure and manufacturing, indicating a long-term growth strategy. However, the need for a correction to the accounts, highlighted by media as a 'typo blunder,' continues to raise concerns about financial reporting accuracy and has contributed to market negativity.
cards.source: ASX: Half Year Accounts (2026-03-11); ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13); simplywall.st (2026-03-16); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16)

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: Following the H1 2026 results and accounting correction, short sellers have reportedly 'circled' IperionX, with Capitalbrief.com reporting Spruce Point hinting at a second attack. This suggests a negative outlook from some analytical firms or market participants, further evidenced by the stock sliding on heavy volume as rising losses trigger a growth story repricing.
cards.tension: While short interest indicates a bearish view, the company's long-term narrative, aligning with the US push to onshore titanium manufacturing, continues to be a counter-argument for some analysts, despite current losses and the intensified short-seller activity.

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: IperionX is described as an American producer of titanium alloys, titanium products, and critical minerals. However, no specific disclosures or performance data from direct competitors in the titanium or critical minerals sector were provided in the available inputs.
cards.tension: Without direct competitor disclosures, it is challenging to benchmark IperionX's operational performance, market share, or strategic positioning against its peers, limiting a comprehensive competitive analysis.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: Global commodity prices are projected to hit six-year lows in March 2026, according to the World Bank. Today's data shows copper and gold prices declining significantly by -4.8% and -6.6% respectively. However, critical minerals are identified as a 'new commodity supercycle' driven by China's economic transformation, and headlines note supply disruptions for other industrial metals like aluminum due to geopolitical events.
cards.tension: While general commodity weakness, reinforced by today's declines in copper and gold, presents a headwind, IperionX's focus on titanium and critical minerals, particularly within the context of the US push for onshoring and global supply chain disruptions, may insulate it from broader traditional commodity price declines and benefit from specific demand drivers.
cards.source: World Bank (investor.wedbush.com); Macro & Commodity Context; discoveryalert.com.au

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: IperionX is undertaking 'heavy reinvestment in infrastructure, manufacturing' and plans a '7x capacity expansion at Virginia facility'. Despite these operational developments, the company remains 'pre-revenue with zero commercial sales' as of its H1 2026 results, a point reiterated in recent news coverage.
cards.tension: Significant capital expenditure and capacity expansion indicate progress towards future production and revenue generation, but the continued absence of commercial sales, highlighted by the market's negative reaction to widening losses, raises questions about the timeline for monetizing these investments and achieving profitability.

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: While no specific academic research papers were provided, the company's strategy aligns with the broader 'US push to onshore titanium manufacturing and processing'. This trend is often supported by geopolitical and economic studies on supply chain resilience and critical mineral security.
cards.tension: The general strategic importance of titanium onshoring is a positive structural trend, but without specific peer-reviewed research on IperionX's technology or market, the direct impact and competitive advantage remain unquantified.

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: Media coverage has been overwhelmingly negative following the H1 2026 results and accounting correction, with headlines such as 'Why IperionX (ASX:IPX) Is Down 23.9%' and 'Short sellers circle IperionX after typo blunder'. The stock plunged 22% on March 16, 2026, and 9.14% on March 19, 2026, with shares falling 42% from its recent March high of $6.93.
cards.tension: The company denied any undisclosed news behind the share slump, suggesting that the market reaction might be an overcorrection based on already public information. However, the current day's +9.5% price increase could indicate a short-term shift in sentiment or a potential buying opportunity if the underlying growth story remains intact.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: IperionX issued a 'Correction to Half Year Accounts' on March 13, 2026, just two days after the initial 'Half Year Accounts' were released on March 11, 2026, as confirmed by ASX announcements. This indicates an error in the initial financial reporting, which was widely reported by media as a 'typo blunder' contributing to short-seller interest.
cards.tension: While the prompt correction demonstrates a commitment to transparency and rectifying errors, the initial blunder in a key financial report, and the subsequent negative market reaction, raises significant questions about internal controls and the robustness of the company's financial governance processes.
cards.source: ASX: Half Year Accounts (2026-03-11); ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13)

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: IperionX issued a 'Notice under Section 708A' on February 23, 2026, typically associated with capital raising, as confirmed by the ASX announcement. The company maintains a cash position of US$65.85 million and has secured over US$60 million in government funding. However, recent trading saw shares fall on 'heavy volume' (144% above 90-day average) on March 19, 2026, as reported by Kalkine.com.au.
cards.tension: The substantial cash reserves and government funding provide a financial runway for operations and expansion. However, the 'Notice under Section 708A' could imply share dilution, and the heavy selling volume on March 19, 2026, indicates significant negative capital outflows, outweighing the positive funding news in the short term.
cards.source: ASX: Notice under Section 708A (2026-02-23); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16 - earnings snippet); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-19)

evidence.alignmentSummary: Recent market action, including a significant share price decline on heavy volume, aligns with the company's reported widening losses and the continued overhang of short-seller interest. While management has responded to an ASX query, the overall sentiment remains negative, reflecting concerns over financial performance and future funding.

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity – they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Q2 2026 DFS economics and timeline to first commercial production
rows.currentReading: DFS pending; expected Q2 2026. No preview data available. Company pre-revenue as of H1 2026.

rows.evidence: Spruce Point Capital second short-seller report publication and content
rows.currentReading: Hinted but not yet published as of March 20, 2026. Represents a live and unresolved overhang.

rows.evidence: Cash burn trajectory and capital raise requirement before first revenue
rows.currentReading: US$65.85M cash; H1 2026 burn approximately US$34.77M (though includes non-cash items). Runway estimated at 12-18 months at current rate.

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Dual ASX/Nasdaq listing: Provides capital market access and visibility but does not discriminate between bull and bear cases -- the listing structure is neutral to the commercialisation outcome.
  • Patented technology claims: The existence of patents is verifiable but their commercial value is unproven at scale; patents alone do not discriminate between N1 and N3.
  • General commodity price weakness: The World Bank six-year-low forecast affects all materials companies; it is a headwind but does not specifically discriminate between IperionX's competing hypotheses, which are driven by company-specific execution risk.
  • Macro US onshoring policy: The structural tailwind from US critical minerals policy is present in all four scenarios; it is a background condition, not a discriminator between them.
  • 52-week high of A$9.10: Historical price levels reflect prior sentiment, not current fundamental value; the distance from the high is a sentiment indicator but does not discriminate between the four hypotheses.

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If DFS delivers NPV materially above current market cap with IRR above 20% and confirms first commercial production within 12 months
conditions.then: Then N1 probability rises sharply; stock likely re-rates toward A$6.00-A$7.00 range as commercialisation timeline is validated

conditions.if: If DFS is delayed beyond Q2 2026, or economics are below market expectations, or release is accompanied by a dilutive capital raise
conditions.then: Then N3 probability rises; stock likely tests 52-week low of A$2.17 and Spruce Point attack becomes more credible

conditions.if: If Spruce Point publishes a second short-seller report and the company provides a credible, detailed rebuttal with independent verification
conditions.then: Then short overhang partially clears; stock stabilises and base case N2 is reinforced

conditions.if: If Spruce Point publishes a second short-seller report containing new material allegations about technology, financials, or governance that the company cannot rebut
conditions.then: Then N3 becomes dominant; stock likely falls 20-30% from current levels and institutional selling accelerates

cards.name: Full Year Accounts FY2026

conditions.if: If FY2026 full-year accounts are released cleanly with no corrections, show controlled cash burn, and include first commercial revenue or a signed offtake agreement
conditions.then: Then governance discount partially reverses; N2 base case is reinforced and N1 probability increases

conditions.if: If FY2026 accounts require further corrections, show cash burn accelerating beyond US$70M annualised, or reveal a capital raise requirement
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case is confirmed; stock likely breaks below A$2.17 52-week low and funding risk becomes acute

cards.source: ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16)

coverageRows.confidence: Medium -- company denials are motivated communications; limited forward guidance provided
Medium -- accounts verified but correction raises internal controls questions; no auditor commentary available

coverageRows.freshness: No formal broker ratings or price targets identified at initiation
coverageRows.confidence: Low -- only signal is negative (Spruce Point short interest); no buy-side consensus available
Low -- no benchmarking possible against titanium peers; competitive position unquantified
Medium -- macro context is clear (commodity headwinds) but titanium-specific price data not available
Low -- no independent verification of Virginia facility progress, capacity utilisation, or technology readiness level
Low -- structural onshoring thesis is directionally supported but no peer-reviewed validation of IperionX technology
Medium -- abundant coverage but overwhelmingly reactive and sentiment-driven; limited independent analysis
Low -- accounting correction is a negative signal; no management track record data, board composition analysis, or insider trading data available
Medium -- heavy selling volume and Section 708A notice are clear signals; but identity of sellers and short interest quantum not confirmed

couldntAssess: Titanium spot price and forward curve (no data provided)
IperionX technology readiness level (TRL) vs. commercial-scale requirements
Competitor benchmarking (ATI, TIMET, VSMPO-AVISMA, or emerging US titanium producers)
Detailed government grant terms, conditions, and milestone requirements
Management and board track record in commercialising mining/materials technology
Short interest quantum and days-to-cover for ASX:IPX and Nasdaq:IPX
Insider ownership levels and recent director buying or selling activity
Auditor identity and any going-concern qualifications
Detailed capex schedule and funding plan for the 7x Virginia capacity expansion
Customer pipeline and any non-binding letters of intent or offtake discussions
gaps.analyticalLimitations: This initiation is constrained by the development-stage nature of IperionX, which means there are no revenue, EBITDA, or earnings multiples to anchor valuation. The absence of broker consensus, competitor disclosures, and titanium-specific price data makes it impossible to construct a rigorous comparable-company or DCF valuation at this stage. The accounting correction introduces uncertainty about the reliability of reported financial figures. The analysis relies heavily on media-reported figures (net loss, cash position) that have not been independently verified against the full audited accounts. The Spruce Point short-seller overhang represents an asymmetric information risk: if Spruce Point has identified material undisclosed issues, the bear case probability is materially understated. All price targets in the position-in-range section are indicative only and should not be treated as formal valuations pending a full W1-W4 model build.

trend.structure: Price in downtrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

priceDrivers.primary_driver: A two-stage collapse driven first by a sharply wider H1 FY2026 earnings loss (net loss more than doubled to US$34.77M, zero revenue) on 11 March, then amplified and extended by an accounting correction filing on 13 March that attracted renewed short-seller pressure from Spruce Point, producing the larger second leg down.

primary: H1 FY2026 earnings miss – net loss doubled to US$34.77M with zero commercial revenue, filed 11 March 2026, triggered the initial -14.3% collapse
Accounting correction (13 March) + Spruce Point short-seller re-engagement – catalysed the second and larger leg down, including the -22.2% single-session collapse on 15 March
secondary: Structural cash burn re-rating: US$65.85M cash against a US$34.77M H1 loss implies roughly one year of runway at current burn, raising commercialisation risk
ASX price query and company response (16 March) – minor stabilisation only, confirmed regulatory concern but did not restore confidence
amplifiers: Pre-existing five-month downtrend from A$9.10 (October 2025) – stock was already technically compromised entering earnings, lowering the threshold for forced selling
Technical oversold bounce and probable short covering driving the +9.54% move on the analysis date (20 March)
Broad market and sector weakness – ASX200 -3.3% and AXNJ sector index -3.6% over the 10-day window provided a marginal headwind
background: IperionX is pre-revenue and dependent on US government grant funding and investor confidence – making it structurally vulnerable to credibility-damaging events
Prior Spruce Point short campaign had already primed the market to react adversely to any accounting anomaly
Dual-listed on ASX and NASDAQ, meaning US session sentiment and short dynamics likely influenced ASX pricing on an ongoing basis
rejected: Broad market weakness as primary driver – ASX200 fell ~3.3% over the period versus IPX's -48%; relative underperformance of -43% confirms this is overwhelmingly stock-specific
ASX price query response as a positive catalyst of substance – the company's grant funding reference did not reverse any material portion of the decline
Results of Meeting filing (19 March) – routine, non-market-sensitive, no bearing on price action

report.title: IPX: Two-Stage Collapse – Earnings Miss Triggers First Leg, Accounting Error and Short Sellers Drive the Second
report.executive_summary: IperionX has lost approximately 50% of its value over eight trading sessions to 20 March 2026, a move that is overwhelmingly stock-specific and best understood as two distinct events. The first leg – a combined -28% over 11–12 March – followed the H1 FY2026 earnings filing showing a net loss more than doubling to US$34.77M with zero commercial revenue. The second and larger leg – a further -32% from 13–18 March, including a -22.2% single session on 15 March – was catalysed by an accounting correction filing on 13 March and Spruce Point's signalled re-engagement as a short seller. The accounting error, regardless of its actual materiality, provided Spruce Point with an irregularity narrative that is disproportionately toxic for a pre-revenue company reliant on investor trust and government grant funding. A +9.54% bounce on 20 March on elevated volume is most consistent with short covering from deeply oversold levels, not a fundamental re-rating. Three material evidence gaps – no broker downgrade data, no ASIC short position confirmation, and no detail on the accounting correction's substance – prevent high-confidence conclusions.
report.full_note:
IperionX shares have fallen roughly 50% in eight trading sessions, from A$7.14 on 10 March to A$3.56 by 20 March, producing 43 percentage points of relative underperformance against the ASX200. The move is best explained as a two-stage event with distinct catalysts of unequal causal weight, not a single earnings-driven sell-off.

The first leg began on 11 March when IperionX filed its Half Year Accounts for the period to 31 December 2025. The result was materially negative: net loss widened to US$34.77M from US$16.24M in the prior corresponding period, a doubling in cash consumption, and the company remained entirely pre-revenue with zero commercial sales. The stock fell 14.3% on 11 March – the worst performer on the ASX200 that session – and a further 14.1% on 12 March as analysts and financial media digested the figures. This two-day move, approximately -28% in total, is directly attributable to the earnings outcome and is confirmed by multiple independent news sources and the ASX filing itself. The timing fit is clean and the magnitude is consistent with a pre-revenue name whose loss trajectory has materially deviated from market expectations.

The second and larger leg is the more analytically interesting question. On 13 March, IperionX filed a Correction to its Half Year Accounts, self-assessed as not market-sensitive. Regardless of the company's own characterisation, Capital Brief reported that Spruce Point – a short seller that had previously targeted IPX – publicly signalled a second attack in the wake of this accounting error. The market did not wait for Spruce Point to publish a formal report. On 15 March, IPX collapsed a further 22.2% in a single session, its worst day of the entire episode. This timing is decisive: the 22.2% move occurred four sessions after the earnings filing and two days after the accounting correction, which is inconsistent with earnings being the primary driver of the total magnitude of the decline. The accounting correction, even if a genuine clerical error, provided the short-seller community with a credibility-destruction framing – accounting irregularities, however minor, are disproportionately damaging for pre-revenue companies that depend on investor confidence, government relationships, and access to capital to fund commercialisation. The second leg from A$5.26 to A$3.56 accounts for roughly 60% of the total dollar loss over the period and is most plausibly attributed to Spruce Point's signalled re-engagement and the accounting correction narrative, rather than the original earnings result.

From a flow and volume perspective, the +9.54% bounce on 20 March on volume of 1.27 million shares – elevated for a stock of this size in a post-shock environment – carries the hallmarks of short covering rather than fresh buying. There is no identifiable positive catalyst in the available data to explain the bounce. Stocks that have fallen 50% in eight sessions with elevated short interest routinely produce violent intraday recoveries as the short community trims exposure into weakness. This bounce should not be read as a fundamental re-rating.

Social media and retail channel data is absent from the available dataset, so the contribution of HotCopper or retail sentiment to either the decline or the bounce cannot be assessed. Given the profile of this stock – a high-profile critical minerals and titanium onshoring story with a US listing – retail participation was likely elevated, particularly in the selling phase. Any retail or margin-call dynamic during a 50% decline would have amplified but not initiated the move. Social signals are assessed as lagging or amplifying in nature.

Three candidate explanations were examined and rejected as primary drivers. Broad market weakness – the ASX200 fell 3.3% and the AXNJ sector index fell 3.6% over the same window – cannot explain a -48% stock move; this accounts for at most 3–4 percentage points of the decline and is clearly amplifying rather than causal. The ASX price query response on 16 March, which referenced US government grant funding of US$60M+, produced a minor +3.9% stabilisation but was overwhelmed by resumed selling the following sessions, confirming it was reactive rather than causal. The Results of Meeting filing on 19 March was routine and non-market-sensitive.

The overall assessment carries moderate rather than high confidence for three reasons. First, no broker downgrade or target price cut data is available for the 11–20 March window; institutional mandate-driven selling at this scale typically requires a formal downgrade, and the absence of this evidence is a material gap rather than confirmation that no downgrades occurred. Second, no ASIC short position data is available to confirm that Spruce Point or other shorts materially increased gross short exposure in this period – the re-engagement signal from Capital Brief is credible but not independently verified. Third, the specific financial content of the accounting correction is unknown; a material restatement would be fundamentally different from a formatting error in its implications for the short-seller narrative. The view would change if broker downgrade records confirmed institutional selling was mandate-driven rather than discretionary, if the accounting correction proved to be a trivial clerical fix with no financial impact, if ASIC short position data showed no increase in gross shorts during the period, or if a positive catalyst not captured in the dataset – such as a US policy announcement or grant disbursement – explains the 20 March bounce.

evidence_quality.primary_evidence: ASX official filings confirmed: Half Year Accounts (11 March), Correction to Half Year Accounts (13 March), ASX Price Query Response (16 March, market-sensitive). Financial results independently corroborated by multiple reputable outlets including Kalkine, SimplyWallSt, Motley Fool, TheBull, and StocksDownUnder. Price and volume data confirmed from Yahoo Finance.
evidence_quality.secondary_evidence: Capital Brief report on Spruce Point short-seller re-engagement is credible financial media but constitutes hearsay-level evidence – Spruce Point's actual published commentary or report, if any, is not in the dataset. StocksDownUnder references a US-listed price drop consistent with dual-listed sell-off dynamics.
evidence_quality.key_gap: No broker downgrade or target price data for the 11–20 March window – the single most important missing variable. No ASIC short position reports to confirm Spruce Point's actual position. No detail on the specific financial lines corrected in the 13 March accounting correction. No NASDAQ-side price and volume data to assess US session dynamics. No HotCopper or retail sentiment data.

what_would_change_the_view: Broker downgrade records from 11–20 March showing mandate-driven institutional selling – this would elevate earnings as the dominant primary driver and reduce the relative weight of Spruce Point

ASIC short position data showing no material increase in gross shorts during the period – this would weaken the short-seller re-engagement thesis and shift more explanatory weight back to the original earnings result

Confirmation that the 13 March accounting correction was a trivial formatting error with no financial impact – this would significantly reduce the credibility of the Spruce Point 'accounting irregularity' narrative and reframe the second leg as pure earnings continuation selling

Identification of a positive catalyst on or before 20 March – such as a US grant disbursement announcement or policy development – that explains the 9.54% bounce as fundamentally driven rather than technical

broker_activity.consensus_change: No broker upgrade or downgrade data available for the 11–20 March 2026 window. Given the magnitude of the earnings miss and the scale of the share price decline, broker downgrades and target price cuts are assessed as highly probable but remain unconfirmed. This is the single most material evidence gap in the analysis.
